description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a crab, featuring a broad, rounded carapace with a textured, mottled brown and orange surface. The crab has two prominent, asymmetrical claws, with the right claw being significantly larger and raised, displaying a rough, segmented appearance. Its legs are jointed and spindly, extending outward from the sides of the carapace, and are covered in a similar rough, brownish exoskeleton. The carapace is adorned with subtle ridges and grooves, and the underside is partially visible, showing additional segmentation. Two small, pointed eye stalks protrude from the front of the carapace, and the overall form is robust and naturalistic, resembling a typical edible crab found in coastal regions. The coloration and surface details suggest a hard, calcified shell typical of crustaceans.
description: A realistic crab with a broad, textured brown-orange shell, large asymmetrical claws, and spindly jointed legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide carapace, both claws (right claw raised), and the symmetrical arrangement of legs.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the large raised claw and the curvature of the carapace.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the carapace, both claws, and the segmentation of the legs.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the smaller claw and the detailed segmentation of the legs and shell.
